what_it_does: A JSONL ledger that catalogs code and design substrate files for a migration-pending wizard run, recording each artifact's kind, domain, role, built maturity, and relative path.
observations: Contains many hundreds of JSONL entries spanning design docs, code, tests, schema migrations, research notes, skill skeletons, and configuration files.
Most entries are marked built: design-only; a minority are code, code-that-runs, schema-migration, generated, or unclear.
Domains include agent-identity, voice-audio, knowledge-corpus, mcp-tools, ui-surface, workspace-infra, telephony, secrets-payment, business-gtm, compliance-consent, and other.
Kinds include architecture, intent-vision, notes, research, design-spec, schema-migration, code, test, config-data, process-meta, glossary-ref, decision-record, scenario-plan, vision, and checklist.
Paths cluster under Visual-DNA-Vault/ElevenLabs-Wizard and packages/elevenlabs-mcp/vault/ElevenLabs-Wizard, with similar filenames appearing in both locations.
Other package areas include packages/vi-invariant, packages/wizard-contracts, and packages/wizard-edge-client.
Special folders include research-briefs, research-outputs, skill-skeletons, _discovery, _system/agent-memory, inbox, outbox/round_00, and docs_needs-organising.
The file is truncated at the end, with the final JSON object incomplete.
standouts: Very large proportion of design-only vault/spec material relative to implemented code.
Presence of a mathematical invariant primitive library (vi-invariant) alongside ElevenLabs integration work.
Built status 'unclear' on a few research briefs, signaling incomplete triage.
Generated OpenAPI schema entry and Supabase schema migrations mixed into the same ledger as design docs.
conventions: Consistent top-level fields: kind, domain, role, built, rel.
rel paths are repository-relative and use descriptive, hyphenated filenames.
built field doubles as a maturity/status tag.
kind and domain use a project-specific controlled vocabulary mixing document type and subject area.
concerns: File is truncated; the last entry is cut off mid-URL.
No per-row timestamp, author, or version.
Duplicate paths/content across Visual-DNA-Vault and packages/elevenlabs-mcp/vault are not annotated as copies, mirrors, or migrations.
built values are not uniformly defined; 'code' vs 'code-that-runs' vs 'generated' vs 'unclear' lacks a visible rubric.
role field is free-text and sometimes duplicates the filename meaning rather than a concise function.
notes: A migration-pending scan ledger that inventories design docs, research, skill skeletons, code, tests, and migrations for the ElevenLabs Wizard / Vi invariant system, with most artifacts still at the design-only stage.
questions: What criteria determine each built value, especially 'code' versus 'code-that-runs' versus 'generated'?
Is the duplication between Visual-DNA-Vault and packages/elevenlabs-mcp/vault intentional migration copies, stale mirrors, or separate versions?
How was the scan produced, and is it exhaustive for wizard-run-1?
Are kind/domain vocabularies governed by a schema or registry?
What is the intended consumer of this ledger: a human, a query tool, or a fusion pipeline?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file path and content indicate it is a scan/index of substrate files annotated with maturity metadata, and that is exactly what it contains.
apparent_themes: Agent identity, invariant primitives, and deterministic reasoning substrates.
Knowledge corpus curation and research-to-spec pipeline.
MCP tooling and generative UI surfaces.
Vault-style organization with discovery rounds, skill skeletons, and system memory.
intention_signals: To create a machine-readable inventory of all intellectual and implementation substrates so a downstream process can query maturity, detect duplicates, and fuse design and code artifacts during migration.
novelty: Treats design documents, research notes, skill skeletons, generated schemas, migrations, and executable code as peers in a single substrate ledger with a unified kind/domain/built taxonomy, rather than separating docs from code.
contribution: Provides a structured, file-local index of substrate artifacts and their maturity, which a fused system can use to locate implemented surfaces, identify speculative design docs, and cluster mirrored or related materials without privileging any single source.
summary_for_embedding: A JSONL scan ledger listing code, design-spec, research, test, migration, and configuration substrate files for the ElevenLabs Wizard and Vi invariant project, annotated with kind, domain, role, and built-status.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog and maturity-tag all design and code substrates in a migration-pending wizard run so they can be queried, compared, and fused.
